fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to north carolina, speech-language pathologist compact north carolina, north carolina speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
board.parent_dept: NC Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in North Carolina.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Complete Education and Clinical Practicum
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA) of the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA) that includes a clinical practicum.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS).

steps.title: Apply for and Complete Supervised Professional Experience (SPE)
steps.description: Apply for licensure and complete a supervised professional experience (SPE) of at least 1260 clock hours within 36 months from the date of initial licensure.

telepractice_text: North Carolina permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: North Carolina issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: North Carolina is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina SLP license cost?
Is North Carolina part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does North Carolina allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does North Carolina have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. North Carolina participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Carolina without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: North Carolina is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in North Carolina
hero.dek: North Carolina Speech-Language Pathologist license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the national exam, and completion of a supervised professional experience.
